purpose: Map every currently available FATHIYA runtime tool to bug bounty discovery responsibilities, prerequisites, and receipt evidence.
tool_rule: Use all relevant tools by role, not all tools blindly. A tool is relevant only when the phase, scope, inputs, and approval profile match.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Confirm which tools exist and what inputs they require before planning.
Check local execution, model, security, automation, and connector readiness.
Produce a secret-safe readiness snapshot across local agents, connectors, Kali, n8n, Zapier, and trading state.
Check one integration such as OpenRouter, Hugging Face, n8n, Zapier, Kali, or broker testnet without exposing secrets.
Fetch program policy, official docs, OpenAPI docs, or user-approved public evidence.
tools.safety_condition: May fetch policy/docs; must not become active vulnerability testing against third-party targets.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Persist approved program policy or official documentation into local knowledge for future retrieval.
tools.safety_condition: Use for documentation and policy ingestion, not exploitation.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Search owned code/config/docs for endpoints, auth checks, feature flags, security headers, and report artifacts.
Verify canonical repository identity and default branch before using repo evidence.
Reason about vulnerability hypotheses, scope gates, validation paths, and report quality without live probing.
Confirm Kali tools are present before creating active profiles.
tools.safety_condition: Inventory only; does not authorize scanning.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Analyze and lightly scan owned loopback lab targets such as Juice Shop; teach tool-to-finding transfer.
tools.requires_scope_record: only if future non-loopback profile exists
tools.safety_condition: Currently restricted to loopback targets by runtime guard.

tools.bug_bounty_use: List connector profiles and whether workflow bridges are configured.
Run read-only connector checks or approved connector profiles such as n8n health/workflows.
tools.safety_condition: External write profiles require approval and a scope/receipt reason.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Check local n8n health before workflow orchestration.
List local workflows that may support approved report routing or intake.
Send approved task/report payloads to owned n8n workflows.
tools.safety_condition: Approval required; no unapproved external action.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Read Zapier and agent-provider inventory to know whether reporting or delegation routes exist.
Discover enabled Zapier apps/actions that could support intake, reporting, notifications, or evidence workflows.
Execute an exact approved Zapier action such as creating a ticket or sending a report notification.
tools.safety_condition: Approval required; do not submit public reports or external writes without explicit routing profile.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Delegate bounded reasoning, code review, or report polish to a ready agent while passing the same scope constraints.
tools.safety_condition: Approval required; delegate receives scope and stop conditions.

tools.bug_bounty_use: Run repository build/tests or runtime tests to validate FATHIYA changes.
Record a decision, missing requirement, or no-tool-needed proof.

missing_external_tool_notes.how_fathiya_uses_it: Can request Burp evidence, project files, screenshots, or manual observations from the operator; can delegate setup only if an approved local/connector profile exists later.
Runtime may confirm installation through kali_tool_inventory; active execution needs a future approved scoped profile.
Useful for local UI and lab inspection; external target interaction still needs scope.

receipt_rule: Every bug bounty task should record tools considered, tools used, why each tool was or was not allowed, and missing requirements.
